pageDescription: Complete guide to survival mechanics for all playable species This guide covers the biological and mechanical differences between Humans, Zrilians, and Robots. Understanding these differences is critical for survival, especially when playing different species or managing mixed crews.

OperationalDetails.title: Species Overview
OperationalDetails.description: Stationeers features three playable species, each with distinct survival requirements.

children.title: Human
children.description: The baseline organic species. Requires oxygen, food, water, and temperature regulation. Vulnerable to toxins (X3 gas) and pollutants.
items: Breathing Gas: Oxygen (O2)
Toxic Gases: Toxins (X3), Pollutant (X1)
Food/Water: Required
Temperature Sensitive: Yes
Organs: Brain, Heart, Lungs

children.title: Zrilian
children.description: An alien species that breathes Volatiles instead of Oxygen. Has different toxic gas vulnerabilities than humans. Requires the Zrilian Species Pack DLC.
items: Breathing Gas: Volatiles (H2 compound)
Toxic Gases: Pollutant (X1) - Note: Toxins (X3) affect Zrilians differently
Food/Water: Required
Temperature Sensitive: Yes
Organs: Brain, Heart, Lungs (Zrilian variant)

children.title: Robot
children.description: Artificial beings that do not require biological sustenance. Immune to atmospheric hazards but dependent on power.
items: Breathing Gas: None required
Toxic Gases: Immune
Food/Water: Not required
Temperature Sensitive: Limited (extreme temps can damage components)
Power: Requires battery power to function

OperationalDetails.title: Human Survival Mechanics
OperationalDetails.description: Detailed breakdown of Human survival requirements and damage thresholds.

children.title: Respiration
children.description: Humans require Oxygen (O2) to breathe. The critical metric is Oxygen Partial Pressure in the breathing atmosphere.

children.title: Oxygen Thresholds
items: Safe: 16+ kPa O2 partial pressure - No warnings, full breathing efficiency
Warning (Orange): 8-16 kPa O2 - 'Oxygen Low' warning, reduced efficiency but no damage yet
Critical (Red): Below 8 kPa O2 - 'Oxygen Critical' warning, efficiency drops sharply
Suffocation: Below ~5 kPa O2 - Suffocation damage begins, unconsciousness follows

children.title: Breathing Efficiency
children.description: Breathing efficiency is calculated as: O2 Partial Pressure / 16 kPa, clamped between 0 and 1.5. Examples: - 20 kPa O2: 125% efficiency (capped at 150%) - 16 kPa O2: 100% efficiency - 8 kPa O2: 50% efficiency - 4 kPa O2: 25% efficiency (critical)

children.title: Pressure Limits
items: Armstrong Limit: 6.3 kPa total pressure - Below this, blood begins to boil
No Minimum Safe Pressure: As long as O2 partial pressure is 16+ kPa, any total pressure works
High Pressure: Generally safe up to several hundred kPa with proper gas mix

children.title: Toxic Gases
children.description: Humans are vulnerable to specific gases that cause toxic damage.

children.title: Toxin (X3) Exposure
items: Warning Threshold: 0.5 kPa partial pressure - Yellow 'Toxins' warning
Damage Threshold: 1.0 kPa partial pressure - Toxic damage begins
Damage Type: Toxic damage accumulates over time while exposed
Recovery: Remove from toxic atmosphere; toxic damage heals slowly over time

children.title: Pollutant (X1) Exposure
items: Warning Threshold: 0.5 kPa partial pressure
Damage Threshold: 1.0 kPa partial pressure
Note: Pollutant affects both Humans and Zrilians but through different warning systems

children.title: Carbon Dioxide (CO2)
children.description: CO2 is not directly toxic at normal concentrations but displaces oxygen. High CO2 levels reduce oxygen partial pressure, causing suffocation indirectly.

children.title: Temperature
children.description: Body temperature must be maintained within safe limits.

children.title: Temperature Thresholds
items: Comfortable Range: 20-30 C (293-303 K)
Safe Range: 0-50 C (273-323 K) - No damage
Cold Warning: Below 0 C - Yellow hypothermia warning
Cold Damage: Below -10 C (263 K) - Burn damage from frostbite begins
Heat Warning: Above 40 C - Yellow hyperthermia warning
Heat Damage: Above 50 C (323 K) - Burn damage from heat begins

children.title: Damage Escalation
children.description: Temperature damage scales with how far outside safe range you are: Cold Damage: Increases as temperature drops below -10 C Heat Damage: Increases as temperature rises above 50 C Extreme temperatures (below -50 C or above 100 C) cause rapid damage that can kill in seconds without a suit.

children.title: Suit Protection
children.description: Space suits provide temperature regulation: - Operating Range: Suits can maintain internal temperature in extreme external conditions - A/C System: Uses suit power and oxygen to regulate temperature - Limits: Extended exposure to extreme temperatures (lava, deep space) eventually overwhelms suit cooling/heating

children.title: Nutrition
children.description: Humans require regular food to avoid starvation.

children.title: Starvation Mechanics
items: Nutrition Depletes: Nutrition decreases over time from normal activity
Starvation Damage: When Nutrition reaches 0, starvation damage begins
Damage Rate (Awake): 0.1 points per game tick
Damage Rate (Sleeping): 0.033 points per game tick (3x slower)
Recovery: Eating food restores Nutrition; starvation damage heals when Nutrition > 0

children.title: Sleep Effects
children.description: Sleeping in a bed reduces nutrition consumption rate by approximately 3x. This is why sleeping through emergencies (when safe) can extend survival time.

children.title: Hydration
children.description: Humans require water to avoid dehydration.

children.title: Dehydration Mechanics
items: Hydration Depletes: Hydration decreases over time from normal activity
Dehydration Damage: When Hydration reaches 0, dehydration damage begins
Damage Rate (Awake): 0.1 points per game tick
Damage Rate (Sleeping): 0.033 points per game tick (3x slower)
Recovery: Drinking water restores Hydration; dehydration damage heals when Hydration > 0

children.title: Organs
children.description: Humans have vital organs that can be damaged or destroyed.

children.title: Brain
children.description: The brain controls consciousness and character identity. Damage Effects: Brain damage causes cognitive impairment and eventual unconsciousness Destruction: If the brain is destroyed, the character dies immediately with 100 brute damage applied

children.title: Heart
children.description: The heart pumps blood and is required for life. Damage Effects: Heart damage reduces overall health regeneration Destruction: If the heart is destroyed, the character dies immediately with 100 brute damage applied

children.title: Lungs
children.description: Lungs process breathable atmosphere and calculate breathing efficiency based on O2 partial pressure. Damage Effects: Lung damage reduces breathing efficiency Destruction: If lungs are destroyed, the character dies immediately with 100 brute damage applied

children.title: Organ Damage
children.description: Critical Rule: When ANY vital organ is destroyed, the entity receives 100 points of brute damage and the entity state is set to Dead. Organ damage can occur from: - Extreme temperature exposure - Toxic gas exposure - Physical trauma - Explosions - Suffocation (prolonged)

OperationalDetails.title: Zrilian Survival Mechanics
OperationalDetails.description: Zrilians share many mechanics with humans but have key differences in respiration.

children.title: Respiration
children.description: Zrilians breathe Volatiles instead of Oxygen. This is their primary biological difference.

children.title: Volatiles Thresholds
items: Safe: 16+ kPa Volatiles partial pressure - Full breathing efficiency
Warning (Orange): 8-16 kPa Volatiles - Reduced efficiency
Critical (Red): Below 8 kPa Volatiles - Efficiency drops sharply
Suffocation: Below ~5 kPa Volatiles - Suffocation damage begins

children.title: Atmosphere Compatibility
children.description: Zrilians CANNOT survive in human-breathable atmospheres and vice versa: Human Atmosphere: O2/N2 mix - Zrilians will suffocate (no Volatiles) Zrilian Atmosphere: Volatiles-based - Humans will suffocate (no Oxygen) Mixed Crew Consideration: Running a mixed Human/Zrilian crew requires separate atmospheric zones or specialized equipment.

children.title: Nitrous Oxide (N2O) Safety
children.description: Zrilians have specific tolerance to N2O: Safe N2O Partial Pressure: Up to 16 kPa Above Safe Level: N2O toxicity effects begin

children.title: Toxic Gases
children.description: Zrilians have different toxic gas vulnerabilities than humans.

children.title: Pollutant (X1) Sensitivity
items: Warning Threshold: 0.5 kPa partial pressure
Damage Threshold: 1.0 kPa partial pressure
Note: Pollutant is the primary toxic concern for Zrilians (similar to how Toxins affect Humans)

children.title: Toxin (X3) Resistance
children.description: Zrilians process Toxins (X3) differently than humans. While not completely immune, they are more resistant to X3 exposure.

children.title: Other Mechanics
children.description: All other survival mechanics are identical to humans:
items: Temperature: Same safe range (0-50 C)
Nutrition: Same starvation mechanics
Hydration: Same dehydration mechanics
Organs: Same vital organs (with Zrilian-variant Lungs)
Physical Damage: Same brute/burn damage handling

OperationalDetails.title: Robot Survival Mechanics
OperationalDetails.description: Robots are artificial beings with completely different survival requirements.

children.title: Atmospheric Immunity
children.description: Robots do not breathe and are immune to atmospheric hazards.
items: No Oxygen Required: Can operate in vacuum indefinitely
No Toxic Gas Damage: Immune to X1, X3, and all other gases
No Pressure Concerns: Can operate at any pressure including vacuum
Advantage: Perfect for EVA work and hostile environment operations

children.title: No Biological Needs
children.description: Robots have no biological requirements:
items: No Food: Nutrition system does not apply
No Water: Hydration system does not apply
No Sleep: Can operate continuously
No Mood: Mood system does not apply (damage does not reduce mood)

children.title: Power Dependency
children.description: Instead of biological needs, robots require electrical power.
items: Battery: Robots run on internal batteries
Charging: Requires battery chargers or droid sleepers to recharge
Power Depletion: When battery is empty, robot becomes non-functional
No Death State: Robots can be recharged and restored, unlike biological entities

children.title: Temperature Tolerance
children.description: Robots have much wider temperature tolerance than organic species:
items: Cold Resistance: Can operate in extremely cold environments
Heat Resistance: Can withstand higher temperatures than organics
Extreme Limits: Very extreme temperatures (lava, etc.) can still damage robot components
No Suit Required: Robots do not need environmental suits for temperature regulation

children.title: Physical Damage
children.description: Robots can still be damaged physically:
items: Brute Damage: Applies normally from impacts, explosions, etc.
Burn Damage: Applies from extreme heat or electrical damage
Repair: Requires repair tools rather than medical treatment
No Organs: No vital organ system - damage is distributed

OperationalDetails.title: Physical Damage
OperationalDetails.description: All species (including Robots) can receive physical damage from various sources.

children.title: Damage Types
items: Brute: Physical impacts, falls, explosions, crushing
Burn: Fire, extreme heat, extreme cold (frostbite), electrical
Oxygen: Suffocation damage from lack of breathing gas
Toxic: Poison damage from toxic gas exposure
Starvation: Damage from lack of food
Hydration: Damage from lack of water
Radiation: Damage from radiation exposure
Stun: Temporary incapacitation (from explosions, etc.)

children.title: Explosion Damage
children.description: Explosions deal damage based on force and distance.

children.title: Mechanics
items: Damage Types: Explosions deal both Brute AND Stun damage
Max Damage: Calculated as: min(force x 100, maxDamage)
Distance Falloff: Damage decreases linearly from center to edge of radius
At Center: Full damage (force x 100)
At Radius Edge: Zero damage

children.title: Common Explosion Sources
items: Gas Canister Rupture: Force scales with pressure, radius scales with pressure
Furnace Explosion: Force scales with internal pressure vs max pressure
Grenade: Force = 1600, Radius = 4.3m (very dangerous)
Advanced Suit Overheat: Force = 200, Radius = 2.3m
Robot Mining Explosion: Force = 200, Radius = 4m

children.title: Protection
children.description: Walls and structures can block explosion damage. Distance is your best protection - even a few meters significantly reduces damage.

children.title: Fall Damage
children.description: Falling from height causes brute damage on impact.
items: Safe Fall: Short falls (a few meters) cause no damage
Damage Threshold: Falls above a certain height begin dealing damage
Scaling: Damage increases with fall distance
Fatal Falls: Very long falls can be instantly fatal
Jetpack: Using jetpack thrust before impact can reduce or negate fall damage

children.title: Lava Damage
children.description: Contact with lava deals continuous burn damage.
items: With Suit: Suit and helmet take damage first
Held Items: Items in hands also take lava damage
Without Suit: Direct burn damage to entity - quickly fatal
Damage Rate: Very high - lava kills rapidly

children.title: Health and Death
children.description: Health is calculated from total damage across all types.

children.title: Health Calculation
children.description:
Total Health = Max Health - (Brute + Burn + Toxic + Oxygen + Starvation + Hydration + Radiation)

All damage types contribute to overall health loss.

children.title: Entity States
items: Alive: Normal operation
Unconscious: Incapacitated but can potentially be revived
Dead: Character is dead - respawn required

children.title: Damage Recovery
children.description: Most damage types heal over time once the source is removed: Starvation/Hydration: Heals at same rate as damage when food/water is available Toxic: Heals slowly over time when not exposed Oxygen: Heals when breathing properly Brute/Burn: Requires medical treatment or slow natural healing

OperationalDetails.title: Survival Tips by Species
OperationalDetails.description: Quick reference tips for each species.

children.title: Human Tips
items: Maintain 16+ kPa O2 partial pressure in your breathing atmosphere
Keep body temperature between 0-50 C (suit A/C helps)
Monitor suit waste tank - vent before it reaches 4052 kPa
Sleep to reduce food/water consumption during emergencies
Avoid Toxins (X3) and Pollutants (X1) - even small amounts cause damage
Keep spare filters - they deplete over time

children.title: Zrilian Tips
items: Maintain 16+ kPa Volatiles partial pressure - NOT Oxygen
You CANNOT share atmosphere with Humans
Watch for Pollutant (X1) exposure - your primary toxic threat
Same temperature limits as Humans (0-50 C)
Same food/water requirements as Humans
N2O exposure above 16 kPa causes problems

children.title: Robot Tips
items: Monitor battery power - you stop functioning when depleted
No atmosphere needed - perfect for vacuum/hostile environment work
Still vulnerable to physical damage and explosions
Can operate in extreme temperatures where organics cannot
Use droid sleepers for recharging
No healing - requires repair for damage
